mods.name: Cold Air Intake
mods.description: A simple intake upgrade for the 1.6L or 1.8L engine improves throttle response and adds a pleasant intake sound at high RPM.
mods.installNotes: Remove stock airbox. Route new intake away from exhaust heat toward the fender well. Ensure no vacuum leaks at the throttle body connection. 20 minutes.

mods.name: Performance Exhaust
mods.description: The stock exhaust is very restrictive. An aftermarket system adds a sweet high-RPM rasp to the small-displacement engine.
mods.partNumbers: Racing Beat REV8 Exhaust, GReddy SP2
mods.installNotes: Bolt-on from the catalytic converter back. Single exit design. Hang on factory mounts. Expect a 5–8hp gain from reduced backpressure. 45 minutes.

mods.name: Lowering Springs / Sway Bars
mods.description: Lower the NA Miata by 20–25mm and upgrade sway bars for significantly improved handling. The NA responds massively to suspension changes.
mods.partNumbers: Racing Beat Spring Kit, Flyin' Miata sway bars FM-SB-NA
mods.installNotes: Springs require a compressor. Sway bars bolt to factory locations. A thicker rear bar reduces understeer. Alignment mandatory after springs. Transforms the car's cornering ability.

mods.name: Performance Header
mods.description: A tubular exhaust header is one of the best power-per-dollar mods for the NA Miata. Gains of 8–12hp on the 1.8L engine.
mods.partNumbers: Racing Beat Header 55211, ISR Performance RS header
mods.installNotes: Remove stock cast iron manifold (heavy). Install tubular header with new gasket. Apply anti-seize to all studs. Some headers require clearancing of the steering shaft. 2–3 hours.

mods.name: Coilover Suspension
mods.description: Proper coilovers transform the NA Miata into an autocross or track day weapon. Adjustable ride height and damping for every skill level.
mods.partNumbers: Flyin' Miata V-Maxx Classic, Ohlins Road & Track MI20
mods.installNotes: Full strut replacement. The NA is light enough that softer spring rates (350 lb front / 225 lb rear) work well for street. Set ride height 15–20mm below stock. Alignment critical.

mods.name: ECU Tune (MegaSquirt)
mods.description: Replace the factory ECU with a MegaSquirt standalone for full engine management control. Essential for forced induction builds.
mods.installNotes: Plug-and-play version wires into factory harness. Requires laptop tuning with TunerStudio software. Base maps available for NA 1.6 and 1.8. Professional dyno tune recommended. 4+ hours for install and base tune.

mods.name: Turbo Kit
mods.description: A turbo kit doubles the NA Miata's power output, transforming it from a momentum car into a genuinely fast sports car while retaining its lightweight character.
mods.partNumbers: Flyin' Miata FM II Turbo Kit, CX Racing Turbo Kit NA
mods.installNotes: Full kit includes turbo, manifold, intercooler, oil lines, and piping. Requires standalone ECU or tunable stock ECU. Lower compression pistons recommended for reliability above 200hp. Budget 20+ hours.

mods.name: Roll Bar
mods.description: A proper roll bar adds critical safety for a convertible used on track. Also significantly stiffens the chassis for improved handling.
mods.partNumbers: Hard Dog Sport M2 Hard Core, Blackbird Fabworx NA
mods.installNotes: Bolts to factory mounting points behind the seats. Some models require drilling. Fits with soft top up or down. Required for most HPDE and track day organizations. 3–4 hours.
